title: To Know There Is a God
description: Recognize and affirm the existence of the Creator.

title: Do Not Entertain Other Deities
description: Do not worship or accept any power as divine besides God.

title: Proclaim Divine Unity
hebrew: שמע ישראל ה׳ אלהינו ה׳ אחד
description: Declare the oneness of God through the Shema.

title: Love God
description: Cultivate deep love for God through thought, feeling, and action.

title: Fear and Revere God
description: Hold reverence for God and avoid spiritual arrogance.

title: Sanctify God's Name
description: Act in ways that honor and sanctify God's name publicly.

title: Do Not Profane God's Name
description: Avoid behavior that dishonors God's name.

title: Pray to God
description: Serve God through heartfelt prayer.
notes: Traditional source for daily prayer obligation

title: Recite the Shema Morning and Night
description: Recite the Shema in the evening and in the morning.

title: Priestly Blessing
description: Kohanim are commanded to bless the people of Israel.

title: Study Torah
description: Engage continually in Torah learning.

title: Teach Torah to Children
description: Parents are commanded to transmit Torah to the next generation.

title: Honor Torah Scholars
description: Show respect for elders and Torah wisdom.

title: Write a Torah Scroll
hebrew: ועתה כתבו לכם את השירה הזאת
description: Ensure Torah is preserved through written transmission.
notes: Traditionally linked to commissioning or writing a scroll

title: Rest on Shabbat
description: Cease creative labor on the seventh day.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shabbat
description: Refrain from melakhah on Shabbat.

title: Sanctify Shabbat with Words
description: Mark Shabbat with verbal sanctification such as Kiddush.

title: Do Not Leave One's Place for Gathering Manna
hebrew: אל יצא איש ממקמו ביום השביעי
description: A classic source tied to Shabbat boundaries and restraint.
notes: Interpreted within laws of Shabbat movement

title: Rest on the First Day of Passover
description: Observe sacred assembly and rest on the opening day of Passover.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Passover's First Day
description: Avoid prohibited labor on the first festival day.

title: Eat Matzah on Passover Night
description: Eat unleavened bread at the Passover seder.

title: Do Not Eat Chametz on Passover
description: Refrain from consuming leaven during Passover.

title: Remove Chametz Before Passover
description: Eliminate leaven from one's possession before Passover.
notes: Basis for bedikat and biur chametz

title: Hear the Shofar on Rosh Hashanah
description: Mark the new year through the sounding of the shofar.

title: Afflict Oneself on Yom Kippur
description: Practice fasting and self-reflection on the Day of Atonement.

title: Do Not Eat or Drink on Yom Kippur
hebrew: כי כל הנפש אשר לא תענה
description: Abstain from eating and drinking on Yom Kippur.

title: Dwell in a Sukkah
description: Live in a sukkah during the festival of Sukkot.
notes: Remembers divine protection in the wilderness

title: Take the Four Species
description: Take lulav, etrog, hadasim, and aravot on Sukkot.

title: Honor Father and Mother
description: Treat parents with honor through speech and action.

title: Revere Father and Mother
description: Show reverence and avoid disrespect to parents.

title: Be Fruitful and Multiply
description: Build family life and continue human partnership with creation.

title: Do Not Murder
description: Human life is sacred and must not be taken unlawfully.

title: Do Not Steal
description: Respect the property and dignity of others.

title: Do Not Bear False Witness
description: Testify truthfully and avoid harmful false testimony.

title: Do Not Covet
description: Do not scheme to acquire what belongs to another.
notes: Addresses inward desire and outward action

title: Love Your Neighbor as Yourself
description: Act toward others with empathy, dignity, and care.

title: Do Not Take Revenge
description: Refrain from retaliatory behavior.

title: Do Not Bear a Grudge
description: Let go of ongoing resentment against others.

title: Leave the Corner of the Field for the Poor
description: Set aside agricultural produce for those in need.

title: Leave Gleanings for the Poor
description: Permit the needy to gather leftover produce.

title: Give Charity
description: Open your hand generously to those in need.

title: Do Not Harden Your Heart Against the Poor
description: Do not ignore or close yourself to poverty around you.

title: Inspect Signs of Kosher Animals
description: Eat land animals that meet Torah criteria for kashrut.

title: Do Not Eat Non-Kosher Animals
description: Refrain from consuming species prohibited by Torah.

title: Do Not Eat Blood
description: Blood is forbidden for consumption.
notes: Applies to domesticated and wild animals

title: Judge Cases Justly
description: Apply law fairly without bias.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice
description: Do not distort legal outcomes.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers
description: Establish systems of governance and justice.

title: Build a Sanctuary
description: Create a sacred place dedicated to divine service.

title: Guard the Sanctuary
description: Maintain reverence and protective oversight of holy space.

title: Revere the Sanctuary
description: Approach holy spaces with reverence and proper conduct.
notes: Applies to the Mishkan and Temple sanctity

title: Do Not Destroy Sacred Objects
description: Do not erase or damage items dedicated to divine service.
notes: Includes respectful handling of holy writings

title: Carry the Ark by Shoulder
description: The Ark is to be borne in the prescribed honorable manner.

title: Do Not Remove the Ark Poles
description: The poles of the Ark must remain in place.

title: Set Showbread Before God
description: Place the lechem hapanim regularly on the sacred table.

title: Burn Incense on the Golden Altar
description: Offer the prescribed incense as part of daily service.

title: Keep Fire Burning on the Altar
description: Maintain a continual fire on the altar.

title: Do Not Extinguish Altar Fire
description: The altar's sacred fire must not be put out.

title: Remove Ashes from the Altar
description: Perform terumat hadeshen as part of altar maintenance.

title: Priests Wash Hands and Feet
description: Kohanim must wash before Temple service.

title: Sanctify the New Month
description: Establish and proclaim the beginning of months.

title: Rest on Shavuot
description: Observe Yom Tov sanctity on Shavuot.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shavuot
description: Avoid prohibited labor on Shavuot.

title: Rest on Rosh Hashanah
description: Observe sacred rest on the first of Tishrei.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Rosh Hashanah
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Rosh Hashanah.

title: Rest on Yom Kippur
description: Observe complete sacred rest on Yom Kippur.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Yom Kippur
description: Avoid prohibited labor on the Day of Atonement.

title: Rest on First Day of Sukkot
description: Sanctify and rest on Sukkot's opening day.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on First Day of Sukkot
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Sukkot's first day.

title: Rest on Shemini Atzeret
description: Observe sacred assembly and rest on the eighth day.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shemini Atzeret
description: Avoid prohibited labor on Shemini Atzeret.

title: Appear at the Temple on Pilgrimage Festivals
description: Pilgrimage festivals include appearing before God at the Temple.

title: Do Not Appear Empty-Handed
description: Bring offerings when appearing for pilgrimage.

title: Rejoice on Festivals
description: Cultivate joy and celebration on the pilgrimage festivals.

title: Slaughter the Passover Offering
hebrew: ושחטו אתו כל קהל עדת ישראל
description: Offer the Korban Pesach at its appointed time.

title: Eat the Passover Offering with Matzah and Maror
description: Consume the Passover offering in its prescribed manner.

title: Do Not Eat Passover Offering Raw or Boiled
description: The Passover offering must be roasted, not raw or boiled.

title: Do Not Leave Passover Offering Overnight
description: Do not leave leftovers of the offering until morning.

title: Destroy Leftover Passover Offering
hebrew: והנתר ממנו עד בקר באש תשרפו
description: Burn what remains from the Passover offering.

title: Do Not Break a Bone of the Passover Offering
description: Maintain the offering's integrity by not breaking its bones.

title: Do Not Take Passover Meat Outside
hebrew: בבית אחד יאכל לא תוציא מן הבית
description: Passover offering must be eaten in one designated place.

title: Do Not Eat Chametz on Passover Eve After Midday
description: Refrain from chametz beginning the afternoon before Passover.

title: Do Not Own Chametz on Passover
description: Chametz may not be possessed during Passover.

title: Do Not Find Leaven in Your Domain
description: Leaven should not be present in one's property on Passover.

title: Tell the Exodus Story on Passover Night
description: Retell the redemption from Egypt on seder night.

title: Redeem the Firstborn Son
description: Perform pidyon haben for an eligible firstborn son.

title: Do Not Oppress the Stranger
description: One must not mistreat or oppress a stranger or convert.
notes: Emphasizes compassion toward those without social protection.

title: Love the Stranger
description: Extend active love and care to the stranger.
notes: Echoes the ethic of empathy from Egypt

title: Do Not Wrong One Another in Speech
description: Do not cause harm through verbal abuse or humiliation.

title: Do Not Wrong in Business Dealings
description: Avoid dishonest overreaching in commerce.

title: Use Honest Weights and Measures
description: Maintain accurate, honest standards in trade.

title: Do Not Keep Fraudulent Weights
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בכיסך אבן ואבן
description: Do not possess deceptive measurement tools.

title: Do Not Delay Paying a Laborer
description: Pay workers promptly and do not withhold wages.

title: Return Lost Property
description: Restore lost items to their rightful owner.

title: Do Not Ignore Lost Property
description: Do not turn away from helping recover lost belongings.

title: Help Unload an Overburdened Animal
description: Assist in relieving an animal's burden, even for an adversary.

title: Help Load a Neighbor's Animal
description: Aid others in loading and lifting burdens safely.

title: Do Not Place a Stumbling Block Before the Blind
description: Do not mislead or exploit those lacking needed awareness.
notes: Extends to moral and practical guidance

title: Rebuke Constructively
description: Offer corrective feedback responsibly when wrong is observed.

title: Do Not Hate Another in Your Heart
description: Reject hidden hatred and pursue honest reconciliation.

title: Love Your Fellow as Yourself
description: Act toward others with the same care and dignity you seek for yourself.

title: Do Not Take Revenge
description: Do not retaliate when wronged.

title: Do Not Bear a Grudge
description: Do not preserve resentment after a conflict.
notes: Calls for emotional release and repair

title: Rise Before the Aged
description: Stand in respect before elderly people.

title: Honor Torah Sages
description: Show deference to elders of wisdom.

title: Do Not Oppress the Convert with Words
description: Do not hurt a convert through speech or social pressure.

title: Love the Convert
description: Extend active care and inclusion to the convert.

title: Use Honest Weights and Measures
description: Maintain accurate standards in trade.

title: Do Not Commit Fraud in Commerce
hebrew: לא תעשו עול במשפט במדה במשקל ובמשורה
description: Do not cheat in quantity, quality, or valuation.

title: Do Not Possess False Weights
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בכיסך אבן ואבן
description: Do not keep dishonest measuring tools.

title: Do Not Possess False Measures
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בביתך איפה ואיפה
description: Do not keep inaccurate dry or liquid measures.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers
description: Establish courts and legal authority in every community.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice
description: Do not distort legal judgment.

title: Do Not Show Favoritism in Judgment
description: Do not favor litigants because of status.

title: Judge Righteously
description: Render verdicts with fairness and diligence.

title: Do Not Accept Bribes
description: Judges must reject gifts that bias judgment.

title: Do Not Fear a Violent Litigant
description: A judge must not shrink from truth due to intimidation.

title: Do Not Testify Falsely
description: Do not give false testimony.

title: Do Not Accept False Reports
description: Do not receive or advance baseless claims.

title: Investigate Witnesses Thoroughly
description: Examine testimony carefully before ruling.

title: Do Not Execute on Circumstantial Evidence Alone
description: Capital cases require proper testimony.

title: Do Not Show Mercy to a Murderer in Sentencing
description: Do not commute punishment when guilt is judicially established.

title: Separate Cities of Refuge
description: Prepare refuge cities for accidental killers.

title: Do Not Take Ransom from a Murderer
description: Do not substitute payment for deserved legal penalty.

title: Do Not Take Ransom from One Fleeing to Refuge
hebrew: ולא תקחו כפר לנוס אל עיר מקלטו
description: Do not permit payment in place of exile to refuge.

title: Break the Neck of an Unsold Firstborn Donkey or Redeem It
description: Redeem a firstborn donkey with a lamb; if not, perform arifah.

title: Redeem the Firstborn Son
description: Redeem a firstborn son from priestly service.

title: Do Not Delay Redemption of the Firstborn Son
description: Do not postpone pidyon haben beyond its proper time.

title: Give the Priestly Gifts from Slaughtered Animals
description: Give foreleg, cheeks, and maw to a kohen.

title: Set Aside First Shearing for the Kohen
description: Give first wool shearing gifts to a kohen.

title: Do Not Work a Firstborn Kosher Animal
description: Do not use a consecrated firstborn animal for labor.

title: Do Not Shear a Firstborn Kosher Animal
description: Do not shear a consecrated firstborn animal.

title: Bring Offerings on Pilgrimage Festivals
description: Appear at the Temple with fitting offerings.

title: Rejoice on Festivals
description: Celebrate sacred days with joy and shared meals.
notes: Includes family and vulnerable community members

title: Do Not Neglect Levite Joy on Festivals
description: Include Levites in festival support and celebration.

title: Assemble for Hakhel in the Sabbatical Year
description: Gather the nation to hear Torah publicly after shemitah.

title: Write and Preserve a Sefer Torah for the King
hebrew: וכתב לו את משנה התורה הזאת
description: A king must keep a Torah scroll with him.

title: A King Must Not Acquire Excess Horses
description: Royal power must not rely on military excess.

title: A King Must Not Take Excess Wives
description: A king may not multiply wives in ways that corrupt judgment.

title: A King Must Not Hoard Excess Silver and Gold
hebrew: וכסף וזהב לא ירבה לו מאד
description: A king must avoid extreme accumulation of wealth.

title: Destroy Idolatrous Altars in the Land
description: Remove established structures of idolatrous worship.

title: Do Not Benefit from Idolatry
hebrew: ולא ידבק בידך מאומה מן החרם
description: Do not derive gain from items dedicated to idolatrous destruction.

title: Do Not Covet What Belongs to Another
description: Do not scheme to acquire what belongs to someone else.

title: Do Not Desire Another's Property
description: Do not nurture acquisitive desire toward another's possessions.

title: Release Loans in the Sabbatical Year
description: Cancel private debts at the end of shemitah.

title: Do Not Refuse a Loan Because Shemitah Is Near
hebrew: השמר לך פן יהיה דבר עם לבבך בליעל
description: Do not withhold lending due to impending debt release.
notes: Protects access to credit for the poor

title: Give Charity to the Poor
description: Provide financial support to those in need.

title: Do Not Harden Your Heart Against the Poor
description: Do not become emotionally closed to poverty around you.

title: Do Not Close Your Hand from the Poor
hebrew: ולא תקפץ את ידך מאחיך האביון
description: Do not refuse material help when able to assist.

title: Support a Fellow Before They Fall into Poverty
description: Strengthen a vulnerable person with timely support before collapse.

title: Give an Interest-Free Loan to the Poor
description: Lend money to those in need without exploitation.

title: Do Not Charge Interest to a Fellow Jew
description: Do not impose usury on loans made within the covenant community.

title: Do Not Cause a Borrower to Pay Interest
description: Do not structure or facilitate lending terms that extract interest.
notes: Includes all parties assisting prohibited interest

title: Return a Pledge to Its Owner When Needed
description: Return collateral at times when the debtor needs it for daily life.

title: Do Not Seize Essential Collateral by Force
description: Do not take critical garments or tools as coercive security.

title: Pay a Day Laborer on Time
description: Provide wages promptly at the end of the work period.

title: Do Not Delay a Worker's Wages
hebrew: לא תלין פעולת שכיר אתך עד בקר
description: Do not withhold earned pay beyond its due time.

title: Allow a Worker to Eat from Produce While Working
description: Permit agricultural workers to eat produce during harvest labor.

title: Do Not Muzzle an Ox While It Treads Grain
description: Do not prevent an animal from eating while it performs threshing work.

title: Help Unload an Overburdened Animal
description: Assist in relieving the burden of a struggling animal.

title: Help Raise a Fallen Animal of Another
description: Help restore a fallen beast and its load to stability.

title: Return Lost Property
description: Actively return identifiable lost items to their owner.

title: Do Not Ignore Lost Property
description: Do not turn away when you can assist in restoring a loss.

title: Use Honest Weights and Measures
description: Maintain accurate standards in trade and commerce.

title: Do Not Keep False Weights
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בכיסך אבן ואבן
description: Do not possess deceptive tools for cheating buyers or sellers.

title: Judge Cases with Righteous Justice
description: Apply fair legal judgment without bias.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice
description: Do not distort legal outcomes from truth and fairness.

title: Do Not Favor the Poor in Judgment
description: Compassion must not override factual and legal accuracy in court.

title: Do Not Show Partiality to the Great
description: Status and power must not influence legal rulings.

title: Do Not Take Bribes
description: Reject gifts that bias judgment.

title: Do Not Accept False Testimony
description: Do not receive or act on baseless testimony in legal matters.

title: Do Not Bear False Witness
description: Do not testify dishonestly against another person.

title: Investigate Witnesses Diligently
description: Carefully examine evidence and testimony before ruling.

title: Follow the Majority in Judicial Decisions
description: In formal court deliberations, legal ruling follows the majority.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers
description: Establish a system of courts and enforcement throughout the land.

title: Honor and Obey the Great Court's Rulings
hebrew: לא תסור מן הדבר אשר יגידו לך
description: Follow authoritative legal decisions issued by the central court.

title: Do Not Deviate from Authoritative Torah Rulings
description: Do not intentionally depart from binding legal guidance.

title: Do Not Curse a Judge
description: Do not verbally denigrate those serving in judicial authority.

title: Do Not Curse a Leader of the People
description: Do not curse recognized communal leadership.

title: Honor Father and Mother
description: Treat parents with tangible honor in speech and action.

title: Revere Father and Mother
description: Maintain respectful boundaries and reverence toward parents.

title: Do Not Strike a Parent
description: Physical violence toward parents is strictly forbidden.

title: Do Not Curse a Parent
description: Do not verbally curse one's father or mother.

title: Be Fruitful and Multiply
description: Build family life and continue human partnership in creation.

title: Do Not Commit Adultery
description: Do not violate marital sanctity through adultery.

title: Do Not Engage in Incestuous Relations
description: Observe Torah boundaries on prohibited intimate relationships.

title: Do Not Have Relations During Niddah
hebrew: ואל אשה בנדת טמאתה לא תקרב
description: Refrain from intimacy during menstruation until halakhic purification.

title: Sanctify Marriage with Kiddushin
description: Establish marriage through recognized covenantal procedure.

title: A Divorcing Husband Must Give a Bill of Divorce
description: When divorce occurs, it must be enacted through a valid get.

title: Do Not Remarry a Former Spouse After Her Intervening Marriage
hebrew: לא יוכל בעלה הראשון ... לשוב לקחתה
description: A first husband may not remarry a divorced wife after she married another.

title: A Groom Is Exempt for One Year to Gladden His Wife
description: A newly married man devotes his first year to the wellbeing of his wife.

title: Perform Yibbum with a Childless Brother's Widow
description: A brother is commanded to marry the widow when applicable.

title: Perform Chalitzah if Yibbum Is Not Done
description: When levirate marriage is declined, release is done via chalitzah.

title: Do Not Muzzle the Process of Chalitzah
hebrew: ככה יעשה לאיש אשר לא יבנה את בית אחיו
description: Do not bypass required release procedure when yibbum is refused.

title: Circumcise Male Children on the Eighth Day
description: Bring sons into the covenant through brit milah at its proper time.

title: Redeem the Firstborn Son
description: Perform pidyon haben for a firstborn son when required.

title: Do Not Delay Redemption of the Firstborn Son
description: Do not postpone the firstborn redemption beyond its proper time.

title: Sanctify the Firstborn of Kosher Animals
description: Recognize firstborn kosher livestock as sacred to God.

title: Do Not Work a Sanctified Firstborn Animal
description: Do not use a firstborn consecrated animal for labor.

title: Do Not Shear a Sanctified Firstborn Animal
description: Do not shear wool from a firstborn animal dedicated to holiness.
notes: Companion prohibition to working the bechor

title: Inspect Blemishes in Firstborn Animals
description: Examine a firstborn offering for physical blemishes before determining its status.
notes: Ensures only eligible offerings approach sacred use

title: Do Not Offer a Blemished Firstborn
hebrew: לא תזבח לה׳ אלהיך שור ושה אשר יהיה בו מום
description: Do not bring a firstborn animal with a blemish as an offering.
notes: Protects the dignity of sacrificial service

title: Redeem Unfit Consecrated Animals
description: Redeem consecrated animals that are unfit for altar service according to valuation.

title: Add a Fifth When Redeeming Dedicated Property
description: When the owner redeems dedicated property, add one-fifth to the valuation.

title: Do Not Exchange a Consecrated Animal
description: Do not substitute one consecrated animal for another.

title: Both Original and Substitute Become Holy in Forbidden Exchange
description: If one attempts a forbidden exchange, treat both animals as consecrated.

title: Value Persons Dedicated by Vow According to Torah Scale
description: Assess dedicated persons by the Torah valuation table tied to age and sex.

title: Do Not Alter Sanctuary Valuations Arbitrarily
description: Do not deviate from priestly valuation standards set by Torah law.

title: Sanctify the Jubilee Year
description: Consecrate the fiftieth year as a Jubilee with special legal status.

title: Sound the Shofar on Yom Kippur of Jubilee
description: Proclaim Jubilee by shofar blast on Yom Kippur.

title: Return Ancestral Lands in Jubilee
description: Restore hereditary fields to original family ownership during Jubilee.
notes: Prevents permanent alienation of tribal holdings

title: Release Hebrew Slaves in Jubilee
description: Ensure Hebrew bondservants return to their families in Jubilee.
notes: National reset of freedom and household integrity

title: Do Not Sell Land Permanently in Israel
description: Do not execute irreversible sale of ancestral land in the Land of Israel.
notes: Land remains God’s and families are stewards

title: Grant Redemption Rights to Relatives for Sold Property
hebrew: ובא גאלו הקרב אליו וגאל את ממכר אחיו
description: Permit close kin to redeem family land sold under hardship.

title: Calculate Fair Price by Years Until Jubilee
description: Set property price according to productive years remaining until Jubilee.

title: Do Not Wrong One Another in Land Transactions
description: Do not defraud in buying or selling property.

title: Support a Brother Who Has Become Poor
description: Strengthen and sustain one who has fallen into poverty before collapse worsens.

title: Do Not Charge Interest to a Fellow Israelite
description: Do not take interest or increase from loans to a fellow Israelite.

title: Let Your Brother Live With You
description: Structure economic conduct so your fellow can continue to live with dignity.

title: Do Not Lend on Interest Terms
hebrew: את כספך לא תתן לו בנשך
description: Do not structure monetary loans to fellow Israelites as interest-bearing.

title: Do Not Give Food Loans for Profit
description: Do not profit from food advances through increase.
notes: Applies ribit to produce and staples

title: Treat a Hebrew Servant With Dignity
description: When employing a Hebrew servant, treat him as a hired worker, not a chattel slave.

title: Do Not Subject a Hebrew Servant to Crushing Labor
description: Do not impose degrading or crushing servitude on a Hebrew servant.

title: Do Not Sell a Hebrew Servant in Slave-Market Fashion
description: Do not sell a Hebrew servant as one sells ordinary slaves.

title: Redeem a Hebrew Relative Sold to a Foreigner
description: Provide redemption pathways for a Hebrew sold to a resident foreigner.

title: Calculate Redemption of a Hebrew Servant by Remaining Years
description: Compute redemption payment proportionally by years until Jubilee.
notes: Ensures fair and transparent release price

title: Do Not Rule Ruthlessly Over a Hebrew in Public Sight
description: Do not permit ruthless domination over a Hebrew servant.

title: Do Not Make Idols
description: Do not fashion or adopt idols.
notes: Foundational ban on manufactured worship objects

title: Do Not Set Up Carved Images for Worship
description: Do not establish sculpted figures or pillars for worship.

title: Do Not Place Figured Stone for Prostration
hebrew: ואבן משכית לא תתנו בארצכם להשתחות עליה
description: Do not install figured pavement or stones for idolatrous bowing.
notes: Restricts ritual architecture linked to idolatry

title: Revere the Sanctuary
description: Show awe and reverence toward the holy sanctuary.

title: Guard the Sanctuary
description: Maintain protective service and oversight of sanctuary duties.

title: Levites Must Serve in Assigned Sanctuary Roles
description: Levitical families should perform their designated Temple service tasks.

title: Priests Must Keep Themselves Ritually Fit for Service
description: Sanctify priests and preserve their ritual readiness for holy service.

title: Do Not Defile a Priest Through Prohibited Corpse Contact
description: A priest must avoid corpse impurity except for Torah-defined close relatives.

title: A High Priest Must Not Become Impure Even for Relatives
hebrew: ועל כל נפשת מת לא יבא
description: The High Priest may not incur corpse impurity, including for family funerals.

title: A High Priest Must Marry an Eligible Virgin
description: The High Priest should marry according to Torah marital eligibility rules.
notes: Marriage restrictions tied to office sanctity

title: A Priest Must Not Marry a Divorcee or Certain Disqualified Women
description: Priests may not marry women disqualified for priestly marriage.

title: Do Not Profane Priestly Offspring
description: Do not create unions that profane priestly descendants.

title: Do Not Allow a Blemished Priest to Serve at the Altar
hebrew: כל איש אשר בו מום לא יקרב
description: A priest with a disqualifying blemish may not perform altar service.
notes: Service eligibility based on Torah criteria

title: Allow a Blemished Priest to Eat Holy Portions
hebrew: לחם אלהיו מקדשי הקדשים ומן הקדשים יאכל
description: A blemished priest still receives and may eat designated holy foods.
notes: Distinguishes service eligibility from priestly entitlement

title: A Priest in Impurity Must Not Eat Sacred Food
hebrew: ונפש אשר תגע בו וטמאה עד הערב ולא יאכל מן הקדשים
description: A priest who is impure must refrain from sacred foods until purified.
notes: Links consumption rights to purity status

title: A Non-Priest Must Not Eat Terumah
description: A non-priest is forbidden from eating priestly terumah portions.

title: A Hired Worker of a Priest Must Not Eat Terumah
hebrew: תושב כהן ושכיר לא יאכל קדש
description: Temporary workers attached to a priest may not eat terumah.

title: A Priest’s Purchased Slave May Eat Terumah
description: A permanently acquired servant in a priestly household may eat terumah.
notes: Household status rule for sacred portions

title: A Priest’s Daughter Married to a Non-Priest Must Not Eat Terumah
hebrew: ובת כהן כי תהיה לאיש זר היא בתרומת הקדשים לא תאכל
description: A priest’s daughter married outside priestly status cannot eat terumah.

title: Return Terumah Plus a Fifth if Eaten in Error
hebrew: ואיש כי יאכל קדש בשגגה ויסף חמישתו עליו
description: One who mistakenly eats sacred terumah must repay principal plus one-fifth.

title: Do Not Slaughter an Animal and Its Offspring on the Same Day
hebrew: ושור או שה אתו ואת בנו לא תשחטו ביום אחד
description: Do not slaughter a mother animal and its offspring on one day.

title: Leave a Thanksgiving Offering Until the Appointed Time
hebrew: ביום ההוא יאכל לא תותירו ממנו עד בקר
description: Consume thanksgiving offerings within prescribed time limits.

title: Do Not Profane God’s Holy Name
description: Do not desecrate the Divine Name through prohibited conduct.

title: Sanctify God Among Israel
description: Act publicly in ways that honor God and uphold holiness among the people.

title: Rest on the First Day of Sukkot
description: Observe sacred assembly and festival rest on the opening day of Sukkot.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on the First Day of Sukkot
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on the first day of Sukkot.

title: Rest on Shemini Atzeret
hebrew: ביום השמיני מקרא קדש יהיה לכם
description: Hold a sacred assembly and rest on the eighth-day festival of Shemini Atzeret.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shemini Atzeret
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Shemini Atzeret.
notes: Festival labor prohibition for the eighth day

title: Offer a Fire-Offering on Shemini Atzeret
description: Bring the designated festival offering on Shemini Atzeret.
notes: Linked to musaf service of the day

title: Rest on the First Day of Passover
hebrew: ביום הראשון מקרא קדש יהיה לכם
description: Observe sacred rest and assembly on the first day of Passover.
notes: Yom Tov observance for Pesach opening day

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on the First Day of Passover
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on the first day of Passover.

title: Bring the Omer Offering
description: Bring the first barley sheaf offering at the start of the grain harvest.
notes: Marks beginning of the Omer count era

title: Do Not Eat New Grain Before the Omer
description: Do not eat from the new grain crop before the Omer offering.
notes: Known as the prohibition of chadash

title: Count Seven Complete Weeks of the Omer
hebrew: וספרתם לכם... שבע שבתות תמימת תהיינה
description: Count forty-nine days from the Omer offering until Shavuot.

title: Rest on Shavuot
hebrew: וקראתם בעצם היום הזה מקרא קדש יהיה לכם
description: Hold a sacred assembly and rest on Shavuot.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Shavuot
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Shavuot.

title: Leave the Corner of the Field for the Poor
hebrew: ובקצרכם את קציר ארצכם לא תכלה פאת שדך
description: Leave peah, the corner of the field, for those in need.

title: Do Not Gather Every Last Gleaning
description: Do not collect all gleanings; leave them for the poor and stranger.
notes: Supports dignity through access to food

title: Rest on Rosh Hashanah
hebrew: בחדש השביעי באחד לחדש יהיה לכם שבתון
description: Observe sacred rest on the first day of the seventh month.
notes: Yom Tov observance of Rosh Hashanah

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Rosh Hashanah
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Rosh Hashanah.

title: Offer the Day’s Festival Sacrifice on Rosh Hashanah
description: Bring the designated offering of Rosh Hashanah.
notes: Temple service of the new year day

title: Rest on Yom Kippur
description: Observe complete rest on Yom Kippur.

title: Do Not Perform Forbidden Work on Yom Kippur
description: Refrain from prohibited labor on Yom Kippur.

title: Do Not Eat on Yom Kippur
hebrew: כי כל הנפש אשר לא תענה
description: Abstain from eating and drinking as part of Yom Kippur affliction.

title: Rest on the First Day of Sukkot (Festival Sabbath)
description: Observe festival rest at the beginning of Sukkot.

title: Rest on the Eighth Day of Assembly
description: Observe sacred rest on the eighth day closing assembly.

title: Light the Menorah in the Sanctuary
description: Ensure the sanctuary lamp is kindled regularly.

title: Set the Showbread Before God
hebrew: ולקחת סלת ואפית אתה שתים עשרה חלות
description: Arrange the twelve loaves of showbread on the sacred table.

title: Do Not Blaspheme the Divine Name
description: Do not curse or blaspheme God’s Name.
notes: Severe prohibition protecting sanctity of speech

title: Observe the Sabbatical Rest of the Land
description: Let the land rest during the seventh year.

title: Do Not Perform Agricultural Work in Shemitah
hebrew: שדך לא תזרע וכרמך לא תזמר
description: Do not sow fields or prune vineyards in the sabbatical year.

title: Do Not Reap Shemitah Growth as a Commercial Harvest
description: Do not harvest spontaneous growth in the regular commercial manner.
notes: Preserves public access and rest ethos

title: Do Not Gather Shemitah Grapes as a Commercial Harvest
description: Do not gather sabbatical grapes in the normal owner-exclusive way.

title: Count Seven Cycles of Sabbatical Years
description: Count forty-nine years to determine the jubilee cycle.

title: Sanctify the Jubilee Year
description: Consecrate the fiftieth year as Jubilee.
notes: Public proclamation of holiness and release

title: Sound the Shofar on Yom Kippur of Jubilee
description: Proclaim Jubilee with shofar blasts on Yom Kippur.

title: Return Hereditary Lands in Jubilee
description: Restore ancestral property to original families in the Jubilee year.

title: Release Hebrew Slaves in Jubilee
description: Free Hebrew bondservants in Jubilee.

title: Do Not Work the Land in Jubilee
hebrew: לא תזרעו ולא תקצרו את ספיחיה
description: Do not sow or reap the land in the Jubilee year.

title: Do Not Harvest Jubilee Vine Growth Commercially
description: Do not harvest Jubilee vineyard growth in standard commercial fashion.

title: Redeem Sold Land According to Jubilee Rules
description: Calculate land sales by remaining harvest years until Jubilee.

title: Do Not Wrong One Another in Commerce
description: Do not exploit others through dishonest pricing or dealings.

title: Do Not Sell Land in Perpetuity in Israel
description: Do not sell inherited land permanently in the Land of Israel.

title: Redeem Houses in Walled Cities Within a Year
hebrew: ואיש כי ימכר בית מושב עיר חומה
description: Allow redemption of a walled-city house within one year of sale.

title: Do Not Divest Levitical Cities Permanently
description: Do not permanently transfer Levitical field holdings.

title: Support a Poor Person Before Collapse
description: Sustain one who has fallen into poverty so they can remain self-supporting.

title: Do Not Charge Interest to a Fellow Israelite
hebrew: את כספך לא תתן לו בנשך
description: Do not lend with interest to a fellow Israelite in need.

title: Do Not Make a Fellow Israelite Serve as a Slave
description: Do not impose degrading slave labor upon an Israelite bondservant.

title: Treat the Hebrew Servant as a Hired Worker
description: Treat a Hebrew servant like a hired laborer rather than chattel.

title: Do Not Rule Over a Hebrew Servant with Cruelty
description: Do not oppress a Hebrew servant with harsh domination.

title: Redeem a Relative Sold to a Resident Alien
description: Assist in redeeming a kinsman sold into servitude to a resident alien.

title: Do Not Bow to Idols or Graven Images
hebrew: לא תעשו לכם אלילים... ואבן משכית לא תתנו
description: Do not set up idols or prostrate to idolatrous symbols.

title: Keep My Sabbaths and Revere My Sanctuary
description: Guard the sanctity of Shabbat and show reverence for the sanctuary.
notes: Pairs sacred time with sacred space

title: Consecrate Values Pledged by Vow
description: Honor vow-valuations by assigning pledged persons according to Torah scales.

title: Do Not Substitute Consecrated Animals
description: Do not exchange an animal sanctified for an offering with another animal.

title: Sanctify the Replacement if Substitution Was Attempted
description: If substitution is attempted, treat both original and replacement as holy.

title: Redeem Consecrated Houses by Adding a Fifth
description: When redeeming a dedicated house, add one-fifth to the valuation.

title: Do Not Sell Ancestral Land Permanently
description: Do not sever ancestral land holdings through irreversible sale.

title: Count Every Israelite Male Eligible for Service
hebrew: שאו את ראש כל עדת בני ישראל
description: Conduct communal census in the prescribed manner for national organization.

title: Arrange Camping by Tribal Standards
hebrew: איש על דגלו באתת לבית אבתם יחנו
description: Encamp by tribes under distinct banners around the sanctuary.

title: Guard the Sanctuary Through Levitical Watches
description: Assign Levites to protect and maintain guard duty for the Tabernacle.

title: Redeem Firstborn Israelites Above the Levite Count
description: Redeem firstborn sons with fixed silver payment where required.

title: Do Not Allow Unauthorized Service in the Sanctuary
description: Prevent non-authorized persons from performing priestly functions.

title: Sanctify the Levites for Sacred Service
hebrew: והבדלת את הלוים מתוך בני ישראל
description: Set apart Levites through the commanded rites for Mishkan work.

title: Purify the Levites Before Service
description: Perform mandated purification procedures for Levites before duty.

title: Dispatch the Ritually Impure from the Camp
hebrew: וישלחו מן המחנה כל צרוע וכל זב
description: Remove persons with specified impurity from the camp zones as directed.

title: Restore Stolen Property with Added Fifth
hebrew: והשיב את אשמו בראשו וחמישתו יסף עליו
description: A thief-confessor must return principal theft plus one-fifth compensation.

title: Test a Sotah by the Prescribed Temple Procedure
hebrew: ועשה לה הכהן את כל התורה הזאת
description: Where applicable, administer the ordeal process according to Torah law.

title: Keep Nazirite Abstinence from Wine and Grapes
description: A nazir must refrain from wine and all grape derivatives during term.

title: Do Not Cut Nazirite Hair During the Vow
description: A nazir may not shave hair throughout the vowed period.

title: Do Not Defile a Nazirite Through Contact with the Dead
description: A nazir must avoid corpse impurity, even for close relatives.

title: Bless Israel with the Priestly Benediction
description: Kohanim are commanded to bless the people with the appointed formula.

title: Offer the Daily Tamid Sacrifice
description: Bring the continual burnt-offering each morning and afternoon.

title: Offer Additional Musaf on Shabbat
hebrew: וביום השבת שני כבשים בני שנה
description: Bring added Sabbath offerings in addition to the daily tamid.

title: Sanctify the New Moon with Musaf Offerings
description: Bring prescribed Rosh Chodesh offerings to mark monthly renewal.

title: Bring Passover Festival Offerings
hebrew: והקרבתם אשה עלה לה׳ שבעת ימים
description: Offer daily festival sacrifices during Pesach according to command.

title: Bring the Omer Day Offering
description: Offer special korbanot with the grain-season transition service.

title: Bring Shavuot Musaf Offerings
description: Offer the appointed communal sacrifices on Shavuot.

title: Observe Rosh Hashanah with Sacred Offerings
hebrew: ובחדש השביעי באחד לחדש מקרא קדש יהיה לכם
description: Mark the seventh-month first day with holy assembly and korbanot.

title: Observe Yom Kippur with Affliction and Offerings
description: Keep Yom Kippur through fasting, restraint, and the commanded offerings.

title: Observe Sukkot Offerings for Seven Days
hebrew: וחגתם אתו חג לה׳ שבעת ימים
description: Bring the full Sukkot offering sequence across all seven days.

title: Observe Shemini Atzeret Offerings
description: Keep the eighth-day assembly with its distinct sacrificial order.

title: Do Not Kindle Unauthorized Fire on the Altar
hebrew: ונתנו בני אהרן הכהן אש על המזבח
description: Use altar fire according to command and avoid foreign, unauthorized fire.

title: Sound Trumpets Over Communal Offerings
description: Blow silver trumpets over offerings on festivals and new moons.

title: Sound Trumpets in Times of National Distress
description: Cry out with trumpet blasts when communal danger arises.

title: Do Not Withhold the Levites’ Due Support
hebrew: כי שכר הוא להם חלף עבדתם
description: Ensure Levites receive their livelihood allocations for sacred service.

title: Give Terumah from Grain, Wine, and Oil
description: Separate first priestly heave-offerings from produce and yield.

title: Give the First Shearing to the Priest
description: Set aside initial wool shearing from flocks for priestly support.

title: Give Tithes to the Levites
hebrew: ולבני לוי הנה נתתי כל מעשר בישראל
description: Provide the first tithe to Levites from Israelite produce.

title: Levites Must Separate Terumat Maaser
hebrew: כן תרימו גם אתם תרומת ה׳ מכל מעשרתיכם
description: Levites must tithe from received tithes and give to priests.

title: Do Not Profane Sacred Gifts Through Neglect
hebrew: ולא תשאו עליו חטא בהרימכם את חלבו ממנו
description: Do not misuse or neglect proper separation of sacred agricultural portions.

title: Prepare the Red Heifer for Purification Waters
description: Prepare parah adumah ashes according to Torah for impurity cleansing.

title: Sprinkle Purification Water on Third and Seventh Days
hebrew: והזה הטהר על הטמא ביום השלישי וביום השביעי
description: Apply purification waters on scheduled days to remove corpse impurity.

title: Do Not Enter the Sanctuary While Impure
description: Do not intrude upon holy precincts while in ritual impurity.

title: Speak Truthful Vows and Fulfill Them
description: One who vows must honor their spoken commitment in full.

title: Do Not Break a Binding Vow
description: Do not desecrate one’s word by violating a valid vow.

title: Annullment of Certain Vows by Proper Authority
description: Permit vow annulment only through Torah-designated legal relationships.

title: Wage Just War Against Midianite Aggression by Command
hebrew: נקום נקמת בני ישראל מאת המדינים
description: Execute commanded national defense actions through lawful leadership.

title: Kasher Metal Vessels Acquired from Non-Kosher Use
hebrew: כל דבר אשר יבא באש תעבירו באש
description: Purge absorbed flavor from vessels through prescribed koshering methods.

title: Take and Guard Oaths in Judicial Matters Carefully
description: Administer legal oaths within Torah process to establish accountability.

title: Do Not Move a Neighbor’s Boundary Marker
description: Do not encroach on another’s inherited land by shifting boundaries.
notes: Protects property rights and social trust

title: Establish Cities of Refuge for Unintentional Manslayers
hebrew: תכין לך הדרך ושלשת את גבול ארצך
description: Designate and maintain refuge cities with accessible routes.

title: Do Not Accept Ransom for a Murderer Liable to Death
description: Do not commute capital murder liability through financial payment.
notes: Affirms gravity of bloodshed in law

title: Do Not Accept Ransom to Spare Exile for a Manslayer
hebrew: ולא תקחו כפר לנוס אל עיר מקלטו
description: Do not substitute payment for the exile requirement in accidental homicide cases.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers in Every Community
description: Establish local courts and enforcement officers to uphold Torah law.

title: Do Not Plant an Asherah Near the Altar
description: Do not plant idolatrous trees or symbols in sacred service areas.

title: Do Not Erect a Forbidden Idolatrous Pillar
description: Do not establish stone monuments associated with idolatrous worship modes.
notes: Prevents imitation of idol cult forms

title: Do Not Offer a Blemished Animal
hebrew: לא תזבח לה׳ אלהיך שור ושה אשר יהיה בו מום
description: Do not sacrifice animals with disqualifying defects.

title: Eliminate Idolatry from the Community Through Due Process
hebrew: והוצאת את האיש ההוא או את האשה ההיא
description: After valid testimony and trial, remove confirmed public idolatry from Israel.

title: Heed the Rulings of the Supreme Court
description: Follow authoritative legal decisions issued by the highest Torah court.

title: Do Not Deviate from Authoritative Torah Rulings
description: Do not turn aside from binding court rulings right or left.

title: Do Not Appoint an Unqualified or Foreign King
hebrew: לא תוכל לתת עליך איש נכרי
description: National leadership must meet Torah criteria and covenantal identity.

title: A King Must Write and Keep a Torah Scroll
hebrew: וכתב לו את משנה התורה הזאת
description: The king is commanded to maintain a Torah copy for continual guidance.

title: Do Not Multiply Horses Excessively for the King
description: A king must avoid military overreliance and pride through horse accumulation.

title: Do Not Multiply Wives Excessively for the King
description: The king must not take many wives lest his heart turn away.

title: Do Not Accumulate Excessive Silver and Gold for the King
hebrew: וכסף וזהב לא ירבה לו מאד
description: The king may not hoard wealth beyond proper bounds.

title: Provide Priestly Portions from Certain Slaughtered Animals
description: Give designated portions to priests from eligible domestic slaughter.

title: Give First Shearing of Sheep to the Priest
description: Set aside first wool shearing for the priest.

title: Do Not Practice Divination
description: Do not seek hidden knowledge through divinatory practices.

title: Do Not Practice Sorcery or Witchcraft
description: Do not employ magical rites contrary to Torah worship.

title: Do Not Consult Mediums or Necromancers
description: Do not seek guidance from spirit-medium practices.

title: Be Wholehearted with God
description: Walk with simple faithfulness and integrity before God.

title: Listen to a True Prophet
description: Heed the words of an authenticated prophet sent by God.

title: Do Not Withhold Testimony When You Know Evidence
hebrew: והוא עד או ראה או ידע אם לוא יגיד
description: One who witnessed relevant facts must testify when required.

title: Do Not Fear to Execute Justice Against Violent Wrongdoers
description: Do not let misplaced pity obstruct lawful punishment in severe cases.

title: Investigate Witnesses Thoroughly
description: Courts must examine testimony carefully before judgment.

title: Punish False Witnesses with Their Intended Penalty
description: Apply reciprocal penalty to conspiring false witnesses.

title: Do Not Add to Torah Commandments
description: Do not present innovations as additional divine commandments.

title: Do Not Subtract from Torah Commandments
description: Do not remove binding elements of Torah law.

title: Destroy Idolatrous Objects and Their Names
hebrew: ואבדתם את שמם מן המקום ההוא
description: Eradicate idolatrous worship artifacts from the land.

title: Do Not Erase or Destroy God’s Holy Name
description: Do not treat sacred names with destructive disrespect.

title: Bring Firstborn Kosher Animals for Sacred Offering
hebrew: כל הבכור אשר יולד בבקרך ובצאנך
description: Consecrate and bring firstborn herd/flock males according to law.

title: Do Not Work a Sanctified Firstborn Ox
description: Do not use a consecrated firstborn ox for labor.

title: Do Not Shear a Sanctified Firstborn Sheep
description: Do not shear wool from a consecrated firstborn sheep.

title: Eat Tithes in Purity and Proper Place
hebrew: ואכלת לפני ה׳ אלהיך מעשר דגנך
description: Consume designated sacred produce under required conditions.

title: Do Not Consume Tithe Grain While in Ritual Impurity
description: Do not eat sacred tithe produce when impure.

title: Do Not Eat Firstborn Offerings Outside Jerusalem
hebrew: לא תוכל לאכל בשעריך... ובכרת בקרך וצאנך
description: Do not consume firstborn sacrificial meat outside the chosen place.

title: Separate a Second Tithe from Produce
description: Set aside annual second tithe from agricultural increase.

title: Set Aside Tithe for the Poor in the Third and Sixth Years
hebrew: מקצה שלש שנים תוציא את כל מעשר תבואתך
description: In designated years, allocate tithe share for vulnerable members.

title: Do Not Demand Payment from a Debtor During Shemitah Release
description: Suspend collection pressure on qualifying debts in the release year.

title: Release Debts in the Sabbatical Year
description: Observe debt remission at the close of the shemitah cycle.

title: Do Not Refuse a Loan Because Shemitah Approaches
hebrew: השמר לך פן יהיה דבר עם לבבך בליעל
description: Do not harden your heart against lending before debt release.

title: Give Generously to the Poor
description: Provide sufficient support to those in financial need.

title: Grant Severance Gifts to a Freed Hebrew Servant
description: Provide meaningful resources when releasing a Hebrew servant.

title: Do Not Send a Freed Hebrew Servant Away Empty
description: Upon release, do not dismiss a servant without support.

title: Rejoice on Pilgrimage Festivals
description: Celebrate before God with household, Levite, and vulnerable members.

title: Do Not Appear in the Temple Empty-Handed on Pilgrimage
hebrew: ולא יראה את פני ה׳ ריקם
description: Bring an offering appropriate to blessing received on festival pilgrimage.

title: Set Aside a Place for Human Waste Outside the Camp
description: Designate sanitary areas beyond the camp perimeter.

title: Carry and Use a Tool to Cover Waste
description: Use proper tools to bury waste and preserve camp sanctity.

title: Do Not Return an Escaped Slave to a Cruel Master
description: Do not extradite a fleeing slave seeking refuge.

title: Do Not Charge Interest on Loans to a Fellow Jew
description: Do not impose interest on qualifying loans to fellow Israelites.

title: Fulfill Verbal Vows Promptly
description: Keep and perform commitments made by speech.

title: Allow a Hired Worker to Eat While Working in Produce
description: Permit laborers to eat produce by hand during active field work.

title: Do Not Place a Worker’s Harvest into a Personal Vessel
description: A laborer may eat while working but may not collect produce in a personal container.
notes: Balances worker rights with owner property rights

title: Allow a Worker to Eat Standing Grain by Hand
description: Permit a worker to pluck grain manually for immediate eating during labor.
notes: Extends food access in field labor

title: Do Not Cut a Fellow’s Standing Grain with a Sickle for Personal Taking
hebrew: וחרמש לא תניף על קמת רעך
description: Do not harvest another person’s crop as if it were your own.

title: Write and Give a Bill of Divorce When Divorcing
description: A divorce must be formalized through a proper writ given to the wife.

title: Do Not Remarry a Former Spouse After She Married Another
hebrew: לא יוכל בעלה הראשון... לשוב לקחתה
description: A first husband may not remarry his divorced wife after she married someone else.

title: Let a Newly Married Husband Rejoice with His Wife for One Year
description: A newlywed husband should devote his first year to building his marriage.

title: Do Not Draft a Newlywed Husband for Military or Public Burdens
hebrew: לא יצא בצבא ולא יעבר עליו לכל דבר
description: Do not conscript or burden a newlywed husband during his first year.
notes: Protects the formative year of marriage

title: Do Not Take Millstones as Collateral
description: Do not seize essential food-preparation tools as a pledge.

title: Do Not Kidnap an Israelite
description: Do not abduct a fellow Israelite for enslavement or sale.

title: Leave Forgotten Sheaves for the Poor
description: When a sheaf is forgotten in the field, leave it for vulnerable people.

title: Do Not Return to Retrieve a Forgotten Sheaf
description: Do not go back to reclaim produce left for the poor.

title: Administer Court-Imposed Lashes Where Required
description: A court must carry out corporal punishment in applicable legal cases.

title: Do Not Exceed the Authorized Number of Lashes
description: Do not administer more lashes than the Torah allows.

title: Do Not Muzzle an Ox While It Threshes
description: Do not prevent a working animal from eating while it labors.

title: Perform Levirate Marriage When Applicable
description: A surviving brother should marry his childless brother’s widow when conditions apply.

title: Perform Chalitzah When Levirate Marriage Is Not Done
description: If levirate marriage is declined, complete formal release through chalitzah.
notes: Provides legal closure for the widow

title: A Levirate Widow Must Not Marry Outside Before Release
description: Before yibbum or chalitzah, the widow may not marry outside the family.

title: Maintain Honest Weights and Measures
description: Use accurate standards in all commerce.

title: Do Not Keep Fraudulent Weights
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בכיסך אבן ואבן
description: Do not possess deceptive heavy and light weights for cheating.

title: Do Not Keep Fraudulent Measures
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בביתך איפה ואיפה
description: Do not keep inconsistent volume measures for dishonest trade.

title: Remember What Amalek Did
hebrew: זכור את אשר עשה לך עמלק
description: Recall Amalek’s unprovoked attack on the weak.

title: Do Not Forget Amalek’s Evil
description: Do not allow the memory of Amalek’s cruelty to fade.

title: Eradicate the Legacy of Amalek
description: Eliminate Amalek’s enduring threat when empowered to do so.

title: Bring First Fruits to the Temple
description: Bring first-ripened produce to the Temple in gratitude.

title: Recite the Declaration Over First Fruits
description: Proclaim the historical confession while presenting first fruits.

title: Rejoice with Sacred Gifts Before God
description: Celebrate divine bounty together with Levites and resident strangers.
notes: Joy as a commanded spiritual response

title: Declare the Tithe Confession in Its Proper Time
hebrew: ואמרת לפני ה׳ אלהיך בערתי הקדש מן הבית
description: State that required tithes and gifts were separated and distributed correctly.

title: Walk in God’s Ways
description: Emulate divine attributes through righteous conduct.

title: Do Not Add to the Commandments
description: Do not present additions as if they were part of Torah law.

title: Do Not Subtract from the Commandments
description: Do not remove obligations from Torah law.

title: Assemble the Nation for Hakhel in the Sabbatical Year
description: Gather all Israel to hear Torah publicly after the Sabbatical year.

title: Each Person Must Write a Torah Scroll
hebrew: ועתה כתבו לכם את השירה הזאת
description: Every Jew should participate in writing or acquiring a Torah scroll.
notes: Embeds personal bond with Torah text

title: Appoint a King Over Israel When Nationally Established
description: Establish covenantal monarchy under Torah conditions when appropriate.

title: A King Must Not Acquire Excess Horses
description: The king may not multiply horses beyond proper need.

title: A King Must Not Return the Nation to Egypt for Horses
description: Do not create policies that send Israel back toward Egyptian dependence.

title: A King Must Not Take Excess Wives
description: The king must not multiply wives in a way that corrupts his heart.

title: A King Must Not Hoard Excess Silver and Gold
hebrew: וכסף וזהב לא ירבה לו מאד
description: The king may not accumulate extreme wealth for himself.

title: A King Must Write and Keep a Torah Scroll
hebrew: וכתב לו את משנה התורה הזאת
description: The king must maintain a personal Torah scroll and study it constantly.

title: Do Not Move Ancient Boundary Markers
description: Do not shift property boundaries to steal land.

title: Do Not Accept One Witness for Capital Cases
description: A single witness cannot establish capital liability.

title: Investigate Witnesses Carefully in Court
description: Judges must scrutinize testimony thoroughly.

title: Punish False Witnesses with the Penalty They Sought
description: Impose on conspiratorial witnesses the punishment they intended for others.

title: Do Not Show Pity to False Witnesses in Sentencing
description: Do not reduce the due penalty of convicted false witnesses.

title: Set Aside Cities of Refuge
description: Designate refuge cities for accidental killers.

title: Do Not Destroy Fruit Trees in a Siege
description: Do not cut down food-producing trees in wartime siege.

title: Offer Peace Terms Before Waging War on a City
description: Propose peace before initiating battle against a city.

title: Prepare a Special Heifer Ritual for Unsolved Murder
description: When a slain person is found and killer unknown, perform eglah arufah.

title: Do Not Ignore a Corpse Requiring Burial
description: Do not leave the executed body unburied overnight.

title: Bury the Dead Promptly
description: Ensure timely burial of the deceased.
notes: Honors the image of God in humanity

title: Return Lost Property to Its Owner
description: Restore lost items when their owner can be identified.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice for the Stranger or Orphan
description: Do not distort court rulings against vulnerable people.
notes: Protects those with less social power

title: Do Not Take a Widow’s Garment as Collateral
description: Do not seize a widow’s essential clothing as a pledge.

title: Leave Forgotten Sheaves for the Poor
description: Do not retrieve forgotten harvest bundles; leave them for the needy.

title: Leave Olive Gleanings for the Poor
hebrew: כי תחבט זיתך לא תפאר אחריך
description: Do not strip every olive; leave remaining produce for vulnerable people.
notes: Builds dignity through access to food

title: Leave Grape Gleanings for the Poor
hebrew: כי תבצר כרמך לא תעולל אחריך
description: Do not take every grape cluster; leave portions for the poor.

title: Administer Flogging Only by Judicial Sentence
description: Corporal penalties may only follow proper court process.

title: Do Not Exceed the Maximum Judicial Stripes
description: Do not inflict more lashes than the Torah permits.

title: Do Not Muzzle an Ox While It Treads Grain
description: Allow a working animal to eat while laboring.

title: Perform Levirate Marriage When Applicable
description: A surviving brother should marry the widow in yibbum circumstances.

title: Conduct Chalitzah if Levirate Marriage Is Declined
description: Complete chalitzah ritual when yibbum is not pursued.

title: Do Not Humiliate Others in Public Conflict
description: Avoid degrading interventions that shame another person.

title: Use Honest and Accurate Weights
description: Maintain accurate business weights and measures.

title: Do Not Keep Fraudulent Weights and Measures
hebrew: לא יהיה לך בכיסך אבן ואבן
description: Do not possess deceptive measurement tools.

title: Remember the Attack of Amalek
hebrew: זכור את אשר עשה לך עמלק
description: Retain national memory of Amalek’s cruelty.

title: Do Not Forget Amalek’s Hostility
description: Do not allow Amalek’s attack to be forgotten.

title: Bring First Fruits to the Sanctuary
description: Bring bikkurim from first produce to the sacred center.

title: Recite the First Fruits Declaration
description: Publicly declare the historical gratitude liturgy with first fruits.

title: Set Aside Tithes in the Proper Years
description: Distribute agricultural tithes according to the Torah cycle.

title: Recite the Tithe Confession in Its Season
hebrew: ואמרת לפני יהוה אלהיך בערתי הקדש מן הבית
description: Declare faithful completion of mandated tithing.

title: Walk in God’s Ways
description: Model conduct after divine attributes of justice and compassion.
notes: Frames ethics as imitation of God

title: Do Not Make Covenants with Idolatrous Nations
description: Do not form binding pacts that entrench idolatrous influence.

title: Destroy Public Idolatry from the Land
description: Remove idolatrous worship sites from communal life.

title: Do Not Add to Torah Commandments
description: Do not present innovations as new divine commandments.

title: Do Not Subtract from Torah Commandments
description: Do not diminish what Torah obligates.

title: Do Not Heed a False Prophet
hebrew: לא תשמע אל דברי הנביא ההוא
description: Reject prophetic claims that contradict Torah.

title: Love the Convert
description: Actively care for and include the convert.

title: Fear and Revere God
description: Cultivate awe that shapes choices and speech.

title: Serve God Through Prayer and Obedience
description: Serve God through worship, devotion, and mitzvah practice.

title: Cling to Godly Conduct
description: Attach life direction to divine values.

title: Swear Only in God’s Name When Required
description: When oath-taking is mandated, invoke only God’s name truthfully.

title: Do Not Test God
description: Do not demand signs in defiant disbelief.

title: Teach Torah to Your Children
description: Transmit Torah diligently within the home.

title: Speak Words of Torah Throughout Daily Life
description: Integrate Torah learning into ordinary routines.

title: Bind Tefillin on the Arm
description: Wear arm tefillin as covenantal sign.

title: Place Tefillin Between the Eyes
description: Wear head tefillin as sign of mindful devotion.

title: Affix Mezuzah on Doorposts
description: Place mezuzah on doorways of homes and gates.

title: Recite the Shema Daily
hebrew: שמע ישראל יהוה אלהינו יהוה אחד
description: Affirm God’s unity morning and evening.

title: Love God with Whole Heart Soul and Might
description: Channel emotion, life, and resources toward God.

title: Do Not Follow After the Heart and Eyes in Sin
hebrew: ולא תתורו אחרי לבבכם ואחרי עיניכם
description: Restrain impulses that lead to moral and spiritual straying.

title: Remember All Commandments and Perform Them
hebrew: למען תזכרו ועשיתם את כל מצותי
description: Keep mitzvot in conscious memory and practice.

title: Wear Tzitzit on Four-Cornered Garments
hebrew: ועשו להם ציצת על כנפי בגדיהם
description: Attach fringes to qualifying garments.

title: Include a Thread of Tekhelet in Tzitzit Where Possible
hebrew: ונתנו על ציצת הכנף פתיל תכלת
description: Add the blue thread component in tzitzit according to halakhah.

title: Sanctify God’s Name in Public Conduct
description: Act in ways that honor God before others.

title: Do Not Profane God’s Name
description: Avoid actions that desecrate divine honor.

title: Rest on Yom Kippur from Melakhah
description: Cease prohibited labor on Yom Kippur.

title: Afflict the Soul on Yom Kippur
description: Observe Yom Kippur self-denial as commanded.

title: Do Not Perform Prohibited Labor on Yom Kippur
description: Refrain from forbidden work on the Day of Atonement.

title: Dwell in a Sukkah During Sukkot
description: Live in a sukkah throughout the festival period.

title: Take the Four Species on Sukkot
description: Take lulav and etrog set in fulfillment of the mitzvah.

title: Rejoice on the Pilgrimage Festivals
description: Cultivate communal joy on festival days.

title: Appear at the Sanctuary on Pilgrimage Festivals
hebrew: יראה כל זכורך את פני יהוה אלהיך
description: Present oneself before God during the three pilgrimage festivals.

title: Bring the Pilgrimage Appearance Offering
description: Bring an offering when appearing at the sanctuary on pilgrimage festivals.

title: Bring the Festival Peace Offering
description: Offer festival peace offerings in a spirit of rejoicing.

title: Do Not Delay Vowed Offerings
hebrew: כי תדר נדר ליהוה אלהיך לא תאחר לשלמו
description: Fulfill vowed offerings without unnecessary delay.

title: Fulfill Verbal Vows
description: Keep and perform commitments spoken to God.

title: Set Aside Firstborn Kosher Animals for Holiness
hebrew: כל הבכור אשר יולד בבקרך ובצאנך הזכר תקדיש
description: Sanctify firstborn kosher herd and flock males.

title: Redeem a Firstborn Donkey
description: Redeem a firstborn donkey with a lamb or equivalent value.

title: Break the Neck of an Unredeemed Firstborn Donkey
description: If not redeemed, perform the prescribed procedure for a firstborn donkey.

title: Give the Foreleg, Cheeks, and Stomach to the Priest
description: Give designated portions of slaughtered animals to kohanim.

title: Set Aside Terumah for the Priest
description: Separate a priestly portion from produce.

title: Set Aside First Tithe for the Levite
hebrew: ולבני לוי הנה נתתי כל מעשר בישראל
description: Give ma'aser rishon to the Levites.

title: Set Aside Second Tithe in the Proper Years
hebrew: עשר תעשר את כל תבואת זרעך
description: Separate second tithe and consume it in holiness.
notes: Observed in years one, two, four, and five

title: Set Aside Poor Tithe in the Proper Years
hebrew: מקצה שלש שנים תוציא את כל מעשר תבואתך
description: Designate tithe for the poor in designated years.

title: Recite Tithe Declaration in Its Time
description: Declare faithful completion of required tithes.

title: Do Not Eat Untithed Produce
hebrew: ולא יחללו את קדשי בני ישראל
description: Do not consume produce before required separations.

title: Leave Pe'ah at Field Corners for the Poor
description: Leave the field corner unharvested for those in need.

title: Leave Gleanings for the Poor
description: Do not collect dropped stalks during harvest.

title: Leave Forgotten Sheaves for the Poor
hebrew: כי תקצור קצירך ושכחת עמר בשדה
description: Do not retrieve forgotten sheaves.

title: Leave Imperfect Grape Clusters for the Poor
description: Leave secondary grape growth for the poor.

title: Leave Fallen Grapes for the Poor
description: Do not gather individual fallen grapes.

title: Let the Land Rest in the Seventh Year
description: Observe shemitah by ceasing agricultural labor.

title: Do Not Work the Land in the Seventh Year
hebrew: שדך לא תזרע וכרמך לא תזמר
description: Refrain from planting and pruning in shemitah.

title: Treat Seventh-Year Produce as Ownerless
description: Allow equal access to shemitah produce.

title: Count Seven Sabbatical Cycles Toward Jubilee
description: Count the cycles leading to yovel.

title: Sanctify the Jubilee Year
description: Consecrate the fiftieth year as holy.

title: Sound the Shofar to Announce Jubilee
description: Proclaim yovel with shofar blasts on Yom Kippur.

title: Return Ancestral Lands in Jubilee
description: Restore hereditary lands in the jubilee cycle.

title: Release Hebrew Slaves in Jubilee
description: Ensure Hebrew servants return home in yovel.

title: Do Not Sell Land Permanently in Israel
description: Do not treat inherited land as permanently alienable.

title: Redeem Houses in Walled Cities Within the Allowed Year
hebrew: ואיש כי ימכר בית מושב עיר חומה
description: Follow Torah rules for redemption of city homes.

title: Do Not Charge Interest to a Fellow Israelite
hebrew: את כספך לא תתן לו בנשך
description: Lend without interest to fellow covenant members.

title: Support the Poor Before They Collapse
description: Strengthen a struggling person proactively.

title: Give Charity Generously
description: Open your hand and provide according to need.

title: Do Not Harden Your Heart Against the Poor
description: Reject emotional indifference to poverty.

title: Do Not Close Your Hand to the Poor
hebrew: ולא תקפץ את ידך מאחיך האביון
description: Do not refuse practical aid to those in need.

title: Lend to the Poor Even Near Sabbatical Release
hebrew: השמר לך פן יהיה דבר עם לבבך בליעל
description: Do not withhold loans due to approaching debt release.

title: Remit Debts in the Sabbatical Year
description: Release qualifying debts at shemitah.

title: Do Not Press for Payment After Sabbatical Release
description: Do not demand payment once debt release applies.

title: Grant a Freed Hebrew Servant Departure Gifts
description: Provide material support when a servant goes free.

title: Do Not Send Away a Freed Servant Empty-Handed
description: Do not dismiss a freed servant without provisions.

title: Love the Convert
description: Show active love and care for the convert.

title: Do Not Oppress the Convert with Words
description: Do not verbally mistreat or exploit the convert.

title: Do Not Oppress the Convert Financially
description: Do not take unfair advantage of the convert.

title: Judge Righteously
description: Render fair and impartial judgment.

title: Do Not Show Favoritism in Judgment
hebrew: לא תשא פני דל ולא תהדר פני גדול
description: Do not bias rulings toward rich or poor.

title: Do Not Accept Bribes
description: Refuse gifts that distort justice.

title: Pursue Justice Persistently
description: Actively pursue just institutions and outcomes.

title: Appoint Judges and Officers
description: Establish systems of governance and law.

title: Do Not Pervert Justice
description: Do not distort legal process.

title: Bear Honest Witness
hebrew: ועמדו שני האנשים אשר להם הריב
description: Testify truthfully in legal disputes.

title: Do Not Bear False Witness
description: Do not give false testimony against another.

title: Investigate Witnesses Thoroughly
description: Examine testimony carefully before issuing judgment.

title: Do Not Favor the Poor in Judgment
description: Do not skew legal rulings out of sympathy for poverty.

title: Do Not Favor the Great in Judgment
description: Do not privilege influential people in court.

title: Judge Righteously
description: Issue rulings grounded in righteousness and truth.

title: Do Not Follow the Majority for Evil
description: Do not support an unjust decision merely because many do.

title: Follow the Majority in Judicial Rulings
description: In duly convened courts, resolve legal questions by majority.

title: Do Not Fear Any Person in Judgment
description: Do not let intimidation alter a legal verdict.

title: Do Not Distort Justice for Converts or Orphans
description: Protect vulnerable litigants from biased treatment.

title: Do Not Take a Widow's Garment as Collateral
description: Do not seize essential property from a widow as pledge.

title: Return a Lost Object
description: Restore lost property to its rightful owner.

title: Do Not Ignore Lost Property
description: Do not turn away when you can help recover what was lost.

title: Help Raise a Fallen Animal
description: Assist another in lifting an animal that has collapsed under its burden.

title: Do Not Refrain from Helping with a Fallen Load
hebrew: לא תראה את חמור אחיך או שורו נפלים בדרך והתעלמת מהם
description: Do not avoid assisting when a neighbor's animal or load has fallen.
notes: Final mitzvah in this dataset sequence
